reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome — native Cyrillic tokenises to non-empty input_ids via the model's own real VitsTokenizer, and the real text-to-speech pipeline returns non-silent audio; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow; page load conformance tested pass), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile, dedicated module Worker). BEFORE building, the checkpoint's tokenizer_config.json was confirmed is_uroman:false (language:"rus", add_blank:true) with a 44-symbol native-Cyrillic vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor/mms-tts-amh). The real text-to-speech pipeline (Xenova/mms-tts-rus, q8) run on native Cyrillic Russian produced a real Float32Array waveform — 107776 samples @ 16000 Hz (6.74 s), peak 0.7213, rms 0.1168, 96.6% non-zero (non-silent), in 7780 ms; the model's own real VitsTokenizer produced 167 NON-EMPTY input_ids on the same text; no ONNX abort. NOT faked, NOT silent, NOT empty.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page. This Xenova export ships tokenizer.json + onnx/, so the worker uses the plain pipeline('text-to-speech') — the real VitsTokenizer, not a reproduced one.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a mono waveform Float32Array at 16 kHz (out.audio + out.sampling_rate). The page reads out.audio, encodes it to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio>, and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Russian speech (peak 0.7213, 96.6% non-zero).
rubric.notes: The first CYRILLIC-script VITS demo in the catalogue — a distinct non-Indic, non-Latin script (own 44-symbol Cyrillic vocab + Russian-trained weights), distinct from the Latin/Arabic/Indic scripts of the other built MMS-TTS demos.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-rus, linked), that it is an ONNX export of canonical facebook/mms-tts-rus (also linked), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB), license (CC-BY-NC 4.0) AND the is_uroman:false native-Cyrillic fact. Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: Because this Xenova export ships the full tokenizer.json + onnx/ subfolder, the 'How the API works' section shows the plain pipeline('text-to-speech', 'Xenova/mms-tts-rus', {dtype:'q8'}) one-liner — the real VitsTokenizer tokenises Cyrillic directly, no manual reproduction needed (unlike the naklitechie Indic exports). CC-BY-NC 4.0 noted in at-a-glance AND footer.

rubric.evidence: The page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (mount + model {modelId:'Xenova/mms-tts-rus', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:38}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-rus so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Russian textarea (lang=ru), Russian sample chips (Вступление/Приветствие/Погода/Новости/Объявление/Пословица), the Озвучить button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→ru) + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built Hindi/Tamil/Marathi/Malayalam mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The model's own VitsTokenizer tokenises the Cyrillic input, so what the model sees is exactly the real pipeline input — no fabricated or mis-tokenised input, and no Latin-input-as-native relabeling.

All buttons have accessible names (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Russian speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); Russian content carries lang=ru for correct Cyrillic rendering/announcement. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) AND desktop (1280×800), both light+dark. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Small ~38 MB q8 download surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in web worker; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis (the text-to-speech pipeline) runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); UI responsive during synthesis; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow flexible layout
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check (desktop clean · mobile clean)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling; Cyrillic script rendering
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; mark language for correct script shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; Russian textarea/chips carry lang=ru for correct Cyrillic rendering.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; lang=ru on Russian content

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label describing it as generated Russian speech; the numeric readout (samples/rate/duration/runs) also conveys the same information as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readout duplicate the visual

openQuestions: The q8 quantised Russian export is small (~38 MB); an fp16/fp32 variant might improve naturalness at a larger download — worth an A/B if a dtype toggle is added.
Does M2M100 en→ru produce natural, well-formed Russian for the multi-model page across a wider set of English inputs?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a dtype toggle (q8 / fp32) so users on fast connections can trade ~38 MB for higher-fidelity Russian audio.
Add more distinct-script MMS-TTS demos with browser ONNX (e.g. other Cyrillic-adjacent or non-Latin scripts as exports appear), using the same verified pipeline() path.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-russian (Xenova/mms-tts-rus, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the RUSSIAN VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; the first CYRILLIC-script, distinct non-Indic non-Latin demo). CRITICAL pre-check: is_uroman:false with a 44-symbol NATIVE CYRILLIC vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor/amh). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: the real text-to-speech pipeline returned non-silent Russian audio (107776 samples @16 kHz, 6.74 s, peak 0.7213, 96.6% non-zero) and the model's real VitsTokenizer produced 167 NON-EMPTY input_ids; no ONNX abort. Because this Xenova export ships tokenizer.json + onnx/, it loads with the plain pipeline() — the real tokeniser, no manual reproduction (unlike the naklitechie Indic exports). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Ladder: basics (type Russian, hear it), practical (Russian narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→ru translate, then MMS speaks the Russian). MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
